description: §52.15.5 (S255) Leak 2 regression — coalesced callable-init server cells (`server @orders = loadAllOrders()`, `server @invoices = loadAllInvoices()`, both unscoped SELECT *) under auth=required are auto-omitted from the anon SSR seed AND their coalesced /__mountHydrate loader route is auth-gated (previously the callable group bypassed BOTH the SSR-seed omission and the I-SSR lint, baking every user's rows into the anon first-paint with no diagnostic). The E-ADAPTER SSR path invokes the REAL compose handler anonymously: neither @orders nor @invoices rows appear in the first paint or `window.__scrml_ssr_state`; a sibling PUBLIC `@widgets` (auth=none) is NOT over-omitted (rows server-rendered + seeded). The I-SSR-AUTH-SCOPED-CLIENT-HYDRATED info lint fires on BOTH callable cells.
rationale: §52.15.5: the /__mountHydrate coalesced loader route is anonymous-reachable (the page auth redirect does not cover it), so under an auth floor it is gated + the whole callable group is omitted from the SSR seed (all-or-nothing — the client _ssrGuard skips the fetch only when EVERY coalesced cell was seeded). A callable loader is opaque to the compiler (not row-scopable), so an auth-scoped callable group is always the omit-and-client-hydrate case.
